notes.note: Marcus Licinius Crassus, the richest man in Rome and a member of the so-called First Triumvirate with Pompey and Caesar. Cicero distrusted him and feared he would obstruct the recall.

notes.anchor: taken from the temple of Vesta to the Valerian Table
notes.note: Terentia, who had taken sanctuary at the shrine of Vesta where her half-sister Fabia was a Vestal Virgin, was dragged to the tabula Valeria, a banking spot in the Forum by the Senate-house, apparently to give testimony about Cicero's confiscated property. The indignity of a Roman matron being publicly hauled about is what wrings Cicero's heart here.
